part_description: Hebrew terms for peace, wholeness, rest, and silence
category_note: Deferred to Phase 2. 'Relational/Social' rejected as too narrow — shalom spans inner wholeness, relational harmony, covenantal completeness, physical wellbeing, and eschatological rest.
note_on_sha.lom_sub_entries: sha.lom has 6 STEP sub-entries (H7965G, H7965L, H7965J, H7965K, H7965I, H7965H). STEP cannot sub-select verses by sub-gloss from the consolidated 240-verse set. All 240 verses are assigned to the sha.lom parent entry (H7965G). The 5 meaning-only sub-entries each carry a word_analysis block with no separate verse records. Check C soft exception pre-approved for all 5 sub-entries.
note_on_sha.lem_sha.lam: sha.lam (to ally, H7999A) and sha.lem (to complete, H7999A) share the same Strong's number and identical verse sets in STEP. Consolidated per researcher decision: sha.lem (to complete) is the canonical entry at H7999A; sha.lam (to ally) is treated as a sub-gloss meaning entry with no separate verse record. Same Check C soft exception pattern as sha.lom sub-entries.

xref_terms.note: XREF only — no extraction in this registry

data_quality_flags.note: Six STEP sub-glosses under H7965 root: H7965G (peace), H7965L (completely), H7965J (friendship), H7965K (greeting), H7965I (well-being), H7965H (Peace [God]). All sub-letter codes confirmed from STEP source file.
Both glosses map to H7999A in STEP. Consolidated as canonical (sha.lem) + sub-gloss (sha.lam). H7999A confirmed.

cha.resh (H2790B, be quiet) and cha.rash (H2790A, to plow/plot) share an identical verse set in STEP. Both are retained as separate entries — they have different primary glosses and distinct semantic domains (silence vs. physical plowing/mental plotting). The verse overlap may reflect STEP returning the combined H2790 root set for both. Flagged for Phase 2 review.

Aramaic equivalent of sha.lom (H8001). No verses provided in source file beyond the word analysis block. Listed in STEP term list but verse extraction appears not to have been captured. Retained as a valid term entry with empty verse array.
Only 6 occurrences. Small verse set included in full.
Only 8 occurrences. Small verse set included in full.
Only 6 occurrences. Aramaic. Small verse set included in full.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: quiet, at ease, prosperous

verses.text: where they found rich, good pasture, and the land was very broad, quiet, and peaceful, for the former inhabitants there belonged to Ham.
I was at ease, and he broke me apart; he seized me by the neck and dashed me to pieces; he set me up as his target;
"Because he knew no contentment in his belly, he will not let anything in which he delights escape him.
One dies in his full vigor, being wholly at ease and secure,
Behold, these are the wicked; always at ease, they increase in riches.
"Rise up, advance against a nation at ease, that dwells securely, declares the Lord, that has no gates or bars, that dwells alone.

The sound of a carefree multitude was with her; and with men of the common sort, drunkards were brought from the wilderness; and they put bracelets on the hands of the women, and beautiful crowns on their heads.
Were not these the words that the Lord proclaimed by the former prophets, when Jerusalem was inhabited and prosperous, with her cities around her, and the South and the lowland were inhabited?'

word_analysis.meaning_summary: to be silent, be dumb, be speechless, be deaf

terms.data_quality_note: See CONSOLIDATION_CANDIDATE flag — verse set shared with cha.rash (H2790A). Both entries retained pending Phase 2 review.

verses.text: The man gazed at her in silence to learn whether the LORD had prospered his journey or not.

and her father hears of her vow and of her pledge by which she has bound herself and says nothing to her, then all her vows shall stand, and every pledge by which she has bound herself shall stand.
and her husband hears of it and says nothing to her on the day that he hears, then her vows shall stand, and her pledges by which she has bound herself shall stand.
and her husband heard of it and said nothing to her and did not oppose her, then all her vows shall stand, and every pledge by which she bound herself shall stand.

But if her husband says nothing to her from day to day, then he establishes all her vows or all her pledges that are upon her. He has established them, because he said nothing to her on the day that he heard of them.
You shall not plow with an ox and a donkey together.

And the people of Israel said to Samuel, "Do not cease to cry out to the LORD our God for us, that he may save us from the hand of the Philistines."

So he departed from there and found Elisha the son of Shaphat, who was plowing with twelve yoke of oxen in front of him, and he was with the twelfth. Elijah passed by him and cast his cloak upon him.

and said to them, "We, as far as we are able, have bought back our Jewish brothers who have been sold to the nations, but you even sell your brothers that they may be sold to us!" They were silent and could not find a word to say.

"Teach me, and I will be silent; make me understand how I have gone astray.
Should your babble silence men, and when you mock, shall no one shame you?
Oh that you would keep silent, and it would be your wisdom!
"Let me have silence, and I will speak, and let come on me what may.
Who is there who will contend with me? For then I would be silent and die.
Pay attention, O Job, listen to me; be silent, and I will speak.
If not, listen to me; be silent, and I will teach you wisdom.
"I will not keep silence concerning his limbs, or his mighty strength, or his goodly frame.
To you, O LORD, I call; my rock, be not deaf to me, lest, if you be silent to me, I become like those who go down to the pit.
For when I kept silent, my bones wasted away through my groaning all day long.
You have seen, O LORD; be not silent! O Lord, be not far from me!
"Hear my prayer, O LORD, and give ear to my cry; hold not your peace at my tears! For I am a sojourner with you, a guest, like all my fathers.
Our God comes; he does not keep silence; before him is a devouring fire, around him a mighty tempest.

But they were silent and answered him not a word, for the king's command was, "Do not answer him."

The LORD your God is in your midst, a mighty one who will save; he will rejoice over you with gladness; he will quiet you by his love; he will exult over you with loud singing.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: to be silent, quiet, still, inactive

verses.text: They said, "Arise, and let us go up against them, for we have seen the land, and behold, it is very good. And will you do nothing? Do not be slow to go, to enter in and possess the land.

The sons of the prophets who were at Jericho drew near to Elisha and said to him, "Do you know that today the LORD will take away your master from over you?" And he answered, "Yes, I know it; keep quiet."

a time to tear, and a time to sew; a time to keep silence, and a time to speak;

word_analysis.meaning_summary: blessing, prosperity, peace-treaty

verses.text: And I will make of you a great nation, and I will bless you and make your name great, so that you will be a blessing.

Esau said to his father, "Have you but one blessing, my father? Bless me, even me also, O my father." And Esau lifted up his voice and wept.
Now Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing with which his father had blessed him, and Esau said to himself, "The days of mourning for my father are approaching; then I will kill my brother Jacob."

the blessing, if you obey the commandments of the LORD your God, which I command you today,

And all these blessings shall come upon you and overtake you, if you obey the voice of the LORD your God.
"And when all these things come upon you, the blessing and the curse, which I have set before you, and you call them to mind among all the nations where the LORD your God has driven you,

And now let this present that your servant has brought to my lord be given to the young men who follow my lord.

for they did not meet the people of Israel with bread and water, but hired Balaam against them to curse them—yet our God turned the curse into a blessing.
The blessing of him who was about to perish came upon me, and I caused the widow's heart to sing for joy.
Salvation belongs to the LORD; your blessing be on your people! Selah
He will receive blessing from the LORD and righteousness from the God of his salvation.
It is like the dew of Hermon, which falls on the mountains of Zion! For there the LORD has commanded the blessing, life forevermore.
Blessings are on the head of the righteous, but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
The blessing of the LORD makes rich, and he adds no sorrow with it.
By the blessing of the upright a city is exalted, but by the mouth of the wicked it is overthrown.
Whoever brings blessing will be enriched, and one who waters will himself be watered.
In that day Israel will be the third with Egypt and Assyria, a blessing in the midst of the earth,

And I will make them and the places all around my hill a blessing, and I will send down the showers in their season; they shall be showers of blessing.

Bring the full tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house. And thereby put me to the test, says the LORD of hosts, if I will not open the windows of heaven for you and pour down for you a blessing until there is no more need.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: completeness, soundness, welfare, peace
senses: 1e2) with God especially in covenant relationship

verses.text: Then the Spirit clothed Amasai, chief of the thirty, and he said, "We are yours, O David, and with you, O son of Jesse! Peace, peace to you, and peace to your helpers! For your God helps you." Then David received them and made them officers of his troops.

Behold, a son shall be born to you who shall be a man of rest. I will give him rest from all his surrounding enemies. For his name shall be Solomon, and I will give peace and quiet to Israel in his days.
In those times there was no peace to him who went out or to him who came in, for great disturbances afflicted all the inhabitants of the lands.
Behold, I will gather you to your fathers, and you shall be gathered to your grave in peace, and your eyes shall not see all the disaster that I will bring upon this place and its inhabitants.'

Therefore do not give your daughters to their sons, neither take their daughters for your sons, and never seek their peace or prosperity, that you may be strong and eat the good of the land and leave it for an inheritance to your children forever.'
Letters were sent to all the Jews, to the 127 provinces of the kingdom of Ahasuerus, in words of peace and truth,
"Dominion and fear are with God; he makes peace in his high heaven.
In peace I will both lie down and sleep; for you alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety.
May the LORD give strength to his people! May the LORD bless his people with peace!
Turn away from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it.
But the meek shall inherit the land and delight themselves in abundant peace.
Mark the blameless and behold the upright, for there is a future for the man of peace.
Let me hear what God the LORD will speak, for he will speak peace to his people, to his saints; but let them not turn back to folly.
Steadfast love and faithfulness meet; righteousness and peace kiss each other.
Great peace have those who love your law; nothing can make them stumble.
Pray for the peace of Jerusalem! "May they be secure who love you!
Peace be within your walls and security within your towers!
for length of days and years of life and peace they will add to you.
Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace.
Deceit is in the heart of those who devise evil, but those who plan peace have joy.
For to us a child is born, to us a son is given; and the government shall be upon his shoulder, and his name shall be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.
You keep him in perfect peace whose mind is stayed on you, because he trusts in you.
And the effect of righteousness will be peace, and the result of righteousness, quietness and trust forever.
Oh that you had paid attention to my commandments! Then your peace would have been like a river, and your righteousness like the waves of the sea;
"There is no peace," says the LORD, "for the wicked."
How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of him who brings good news, who publishes peace, who brings good news of happiness, who publishes salvation, who says to Zion, "Your God reigns."
But he was pierced for our transgressions; he was crushed for our iniquities; upon him was the chastisement that brought us peace, and with his wounds we are healed.
For the mountains may depart and the hills be removed, but my steadfast love shall not depart from you, and my covenant of peace shall not be removed, says the LORD, who has compassion on you.
creating the fruit of the lips. Peace, peace, to the far and to the near, says the LORD, and I will heal him.
There is no peace, says my God, for the wicked.
They have healed the wound of my people lightly, saying, 'Peace, peace,' when there is no peace.
But seek the welfare of the city where I have sent you into exile, and pray to the LORD on its behalf, for in its welfare you will find your welfare.
For I know the plans I have for you, declares the LORD, plans for welfare and not for evil, to give you a future and a hope.
Behold, I will bring to it health and healing, and I will heal them and reveal to them abundance of prosperity and security.
my soul is bereft of peace; I have forgotten what happiness is;
"I will make with them a covenant of peace and banish wild beasts from the land, so that they may dwell securely in the wilderness and sleep in the woods.
I will make a covenant of peace with them. It shall be an everlasting covenant with them. And I will set them in their land and multiply them, and will set my sanctuary in their midst forevermore.
And he shall be their peace. When the Assyrian comes into our land and treads in our palaces, then we will raise against him seven shepherds and eight princes of men;
The latter glory of this house shall be greater than the former, says the LORD of hosts. And in this place I will give peace, declares the LORD of hosts.'

terms.verse_extraction_note: Meaning-only sub-entry. This sub-gloss is a divine-name marker — the name YHWH-Shalom derives its meaning from this root. Full verse set not extracted (10,284 OT + 22 NT occurrences under the broader deity term). Theologically linked to Part 3 anchor terms.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: The Deity; this name means completeness, peace
word_analysis.theological_note: Peace [God] references sha.lom as the semantic root of the divine name YHWH-Shalom (Judg 6:24). Connects to Part 3 theological anchor ye.ho.vah sha.lom.
senses: Used as a divine title component meaning completeness, peace
Judg 6:24 — sole OT instance of the compound name YHWH-Shalom

word_analysis.meaning_summary: welfare, prosperity, peace, well-being — Aramaic equivalent of sha.lom
senses: Aramaic of sha.lom (H7965): welfare, prosperity, peace, well-being

terms.data_quality_note: See THIN_DATA flag. No verses captured in source file. Aramaic term appearing in Ezra/Daniel epistolary greetings. Verse extraction may need to be revisited in a follow-up STEP session.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: peace offering, requital, sacrifice for alliance or friendship

but the person who eats of the flesh of the sacrifice of the LORD's peace offerings while an uncleanness is on him, that person shall be cut off from his people.

and he shall bring his gift to the LORD, one male lamb a year old without blemish for a burnt offering, and one ewe lamb a year old without blemish as a sin offering, and one ram without blemish as a peace offering,

word_analysis.meaning_summary: at ease, quiet, secure; can also denote careless arrogance
senses: adj 1b) at ease, careless, wanton, arrogant

verses.text: Because you have raged against me and your complacency has come into my ears, I will put my hook in your nose and my bit in your mouth, and I will turn you back on the way by which you came.
In the thought of one who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune; it is ready for those whose feet slip.
Our soul has had more than enough of the scorn of those who are at ease, of the contempt of the proud.
Rise up, you women who are at ease, hear my voice; you complacent daughters, give ear to my speech.
Tremble, you women who are at ease, shudder, you complacent ones; strip, and make yourselves bare, and tie sackcloth around your waist.
My people will abide in a peaceful habitation, in secure dwellings, and in quiet resting places.
Behold Zion, the city of our appointed feasts! Your eyes will see Jerusalem, an untroubled habitation, an immovable tent, whose stakes will never be plucked up, nor will any of its cords be broken.
Because you have raged against me and your complacency has come to my ears, I will put my hook in your nose and my bit in your mouth, and I will turn you back on the way by which you came.
"Woe to those who are at ease in Zion, and to those who feel secure on the mountain of Samaria, the notable men of the first of the nations, to whom the house of Israel comes!
And I am exceedingly angry with the nations that are at ease; for while I was angry but a little, they furthered the disaster.

terms.verse_extraction_note: sha.lam (to ally) treated as sub-gloss of sha.lem (to complete) per researcher decision. Both carry H7999A with identical verse sets. Sub-gloss meaning-only block, no separate verses.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: to be complete, be sound; to restore, recompense, make peace (as act of completing a relationship)
senses: 1a1) to be complete, be finished, be ended
1b3) to make whole or good, restore, make compensation

sub_gloss_sha.lam.meaning_note: The relational/diplomatic application of sha.lem — to make peace with, to come to terms, to enter alliance. Core semantic: bringing a relationship to completeness.

the owner of the pit shall make restoration. He shall give money to its owner, and the dead beast shall be his.

who confirms the word of his servant and fulfills the counsel of his messengers, who says of Jerusalem, 'She shall be inhabited,' and of the cities of Judah, 'They shall be built, and I will raise up their ruins';

and there came a messenger to Job and said, "The oxen were plowing and the donkeys feeding beside them,

with perverted heart devises evil, continually sowing discord;
a heart that devises wicked plans, feet that make haste to run to evil,

Ephraim was a trained calf that loved to thresh, and I spared her fair neck; but I will put Ephraim to the yoke; Judah must plow; Jacob must harrow for himself.
You have plowed iniquity; you have reaped injustice; you have eaten the fruit of lies. Because you have trusted in your own way and in the multitude of your warriors,
Do horses run on rocks? Does one plow there with oxen? But you have turned justice into poison and the fruit of righteousness into wormwood —
Therefore because of you Zion shall be plowed as a field; Jerusalem shall become a heap of ruins, and the mountain of the house a wooded height.
You who are of purer eyes than to see evil and cannot look at wrong, why do you idly look at traitors and remain silent when the wicked swallows up the man more righteous than he?
The LORD your God is in your midst, a mighty one who will save; he will rejoice over you with gladness; he will quiet you by his love; he will exult over you with loud singing.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: to be quiet, be tranquil, be at peace, rest, lie still, be undisturbed

verses.text: So Joshua took the whole land, according to all that the LORD had spoken to Moses. And Joshua gave it for an inheritance to Israel according to their tribal allotments. And the land had rest from war.
So the land had rest for forty years. Then Othniel the son of Kenaz died.
So Moab was subdued that day under the hand of Israel. And the land had rest for eighty years.
"So may all your enemies perish, O LORD! But your friends be like the sun as he rises in his might." And the land had rest for forty years.
So Midian was subdued before the people of Israel, and they raised their heads no more. And the land had rest for forty years in the days of Gideon.

Then the five men departed and came to Laish and saw the people who were there, how they lived in security, after the manner of the Sidonians, quiet and unsuspecting, lacking nothing that is in the earth and possessing wealth, and how they were far from the Sidonians and had no dealings with anyone.

But the people of Dan took what Micah had made, and the priest who belonged to him, and they came to Laish, to a people quiet and unsuspecting, and struck them with the edge of the sword and burned the city with fire.

He built fortified cities in Judah, for the land had rest. He had no war in those years, for the LORD gave him peace.
So the realm of Jehoshaphat was quiet, for his God gave him rest all around.
For then I would have lain down and been quiet; I would have slept; then I would have been at rest,

O God, do not keep silence; do not hold your peace or be still, O God!
to give him rest from days of trouble, until a pit is dug for the wicked.
